Bug / Task Description: Deletions made on one node do not propagate through Merkle anti-entropy to peers. The Merkle tree built from a store silently omits tombstoned keys, so deleted entries are invisible to the diff algorithm and peers never learn about them. Separately, when a tombstone and a live entry for the same key exist at the same version number, the live entry always wins, violating delete-wins semantics. Fix the Merkle anti-entropy path in pkg/sim and the tombstone acceptance logic in pkg/gossip so that deletions converge across all replicas using only Merkle sync rounds.
Commit Subject: fix(merkle): tombstones invisible to Merkle anti-entropy; delete-wins at equal version
